name: Thyme-Fig Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the apple juice, thyme, and figs in a bowl. Set aside for 10 minutes.
2. Stir together the flour, cornmeal, baking powder, and baking soda in a separate bowl.
3. Beat the oil, sugar, and egg until well blended.
4. Pour the egg mixture into the dry ingredients. Add the pine nuts and fig mixture.
5. Mix well to combine all ingredients.
6. Pour the batter into an oiled and floured 9-inch round (or 8 inch square) baking pan.
7. Bake in a 350°F oven for 40 to 50 minutes.
8. Cool for 5 minutes in the pan, then remove from the pan and cool thoroughly.
WARNING: Ensure the recipe remains accurate to the original without inventing ingredients, changing quantities, or altering core cooking instructions.
